title: Loop Intent Language (LIL) Schema
description: JSON schema for Aura AI assistant intents in Loop OS. The Capability Broker mediates all resource access; required_capabilities never leaves the broker's trust domain.

intent.description: The action the user wants to perform

params.description: Parameters specific to each intent. Validation per intent handled by broker logic. Typical fields by intent: open -> {target: string, app_id?: string}, compose -> {type: string, content: string}, search -> {query: string, scope?: string}, move -> {source: string, destination: string}, copy -> {source: string, destination: string}, delete -> {target: string}, send -> {to: string[], subject?: string, body?: string, attachments?: string[], schedule?: string}, schedule -> {title: string, start: string, end?: string, calendar_id?: string}, query -> {question: string, source?: string}, configure -> {setting: string, value: any}, install -> {app_id: string, source?: string}, uninstall -> {app_id: string}

required_capabilities.description: List of capability declarations Aura must possess for intent authorization. The broker verifies these capabilities before mediation.

type.description: Capability type that Aura must hold

identifier.description: Scoped identifier for the resource. For filesystem: path; for network: domain; for app: app_id. For contacts, calendar: the filter string or calendar ID (use '*' or 'all' for unrestricted). For location, clipboard, device, and orchestrate: use an empty string. Must be empty string for types that do not require scoping.

confirmation_required.description: If true, Aura must ask user for explicit confirmation before broker executes the intent. Should be true for destructive or sensitive actions.

context.description: Extra contextual information that might help the broker or Aura's logging, like natural language echo, session id, etc. Not required for execution.
